segments.title: KA-27 Helix as rugged Soviet carrier ASW helicopter
segments.summary: The episode frames the KA-27 Helix as a rugged Soviet answer to shipborne anti-submarine warfare for Kiev-era carrier aviation.
segments.body: The KA-27 replaced an aging KA-25, entered service before all fine-tuning was complete, and gave Soviet carriers and escorts a practical paired-helicopter ASW tool.

evidence.note: The opening defines the design requirement around tracking and destroying submarines far from port in difficult sea conditions.
The transcript ties KA-27 service entry to the Kiev-class carriers and the need for embarked capability.
The tactics section explains paired or three-aircraft ASW operations with detector and destroyer roles.
